title: vigna marina subsp. oblonga
intro: Vigna marina subsp. oblonga, also known as beach bean or beach pea, is a plant species belonging to the legume family, characterized by its climbing habit and oblong-shaped seeds. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which contain isoflavones, saponins, and flavonoids, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Vigna marina subsp. oblonga include the potential to manage cardiovascular diseases, diabetes, and certain types of cancer, as well as its ability to improve bone health and reduce menopausal symptoms. Traditional herbal preparations of this plant involve drying and powdering the seeds, which can be used in teas, capsules, or infusions to leverage its medicinal properties.
